mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, forecasting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inflationary periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge (Sharpe < 0.2) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding 0.4% month-over-month forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate (>58%) during high-inflation months, or the signal fails to predict direction in >70% of out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting higher earnings expectations and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue fundamentals, driving positive short-term market returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 revisions consistently reverse the signal direction, or 5-day forward returns show zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a manufacturing downturn that reduces capacity utilization and corporate profit margins, forecasting negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in >65% of double-decline episodes, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of fiscal stimulus.
